source.page_label: Hunting Seasons | Oklahoma Department of Wildlife Conservation
source.update_note: 2026-27 season data from the ODWC Hunting Seasons page and species-specific regulation pages (pheasant, quail). License fees from ODWC License Fees page.

species.name: Quail (Bobwhite & Scaled)
species.hunting_units: Statewide (some WMAs closed to nonresidents after Jan 31)

species.name: Ring-necked Pheasant
species.hunting_units: 13 counties: Alfalfa, Beaver, Cimarron, Garfield, Grant, Harper, Kay, Major, Noble, Osage, Texas, Woods, Woodward; plus portions of Blaine, Dewey, Ellis, Kingfisher, Logan counties north of SH-51

licenses.name: Annual Hunting License (Resident, age 18+)
licenses.covers: Base hunting license required for all resident hunters

licenses.name: Annual Hunting License (Nonresident, age 18+)
licenses.covers: Base hunting license required for all nonresident hunters

licenses.name: Nonresident 5-Day Hunting License
licenses.covers: 5 days of choice (NOT valid for quail)

licenses.name: Youth Annual Super Hunting License (age 17 & under)
licenses.covers: Deer, turkey, waterfowl, elk, bear, antelope, furbearers, trapping

licenses.name: Nonresident Game Bird Permit
licenses.covers: Required for nonresidents hunting game birds on WMAs

licenses.name: Wildlife Conservation Passport (Resident, age 18+)
licenses.covers: Required for access to Dept. lands unless exempt

key_notes.title: Only cock (male) pheasants may be taken. Evidence of sex (head or one foot) must remain attached until the bird reaches its final destination.

sources.label: ODWC — Pheasant Regulations
sources.evidence: Cocks only: Two cocks daily; four in possession after the first day. Evidence of sex (head or one foot) must remain on the bird until final destination.

key_notes.title: Pheasant season is limited to 13 specified panhandle and northern Oklahoma counties plus portions of 5 additional counties north of State Highway 51.

sources.label: ODWC — Pheasant Regulations
sources.evidence: Open areas include Alfalfa, Beaver, Cimarron, Garfield, Grant, Harper, Kay, Major, Noble, Osage, Texas, Woods, Woodward counties; and portions of Blaine, Dewey, Ellis, Kingfisher and Logan counties north of State Highway 51.

key_notes.title: "Pot shooting" quail (shooting birds while resting on the ground) is illegal in Oklahoma.

sources.label: ODWC — Quail Regulations
sources.evidence: At no time may any quail or covey be shot while resting on the ground, commonly called 'pot shooting.'

key_notes.title: Nonresidents hunting game birds on a Wildlife Management Area (WMA) must possess a Nonresident Game Bird Permit ($100).

sources.label: ODWC — License Fees
sources.evidence: Nonresident Game Bird Permit: $100. Required for nonresidents hunting game birds on any WMA, GMA, PHA, WRP, WDU, or WMU.

key_notes.title: The nonresident 5-day hunting license is NOT valid for hunting quail.

sources.label: ODWC — Quail Regulations
sources.evidence: The adult nonresident 5-day hunting license is not valid for hunting quail.
